today.prep_notes: Confirm Group Bets design status with Miguel before any review. If design is not complete (Miguel may have needed to wait for Jessica's input), reset the timeline immediately — 11 June hard deadline leaves no slack. Jessica is off today so any review is without her. Note: GDCU-8619 (Operator List Redesign) was unblocked on 07/05 — update Ciara on next milestone expectations for that work too.

action_items.action: JESSICA OFF TODAY — She messaged Mike this morning requesting a leave day. GDCU-8948 (Update Ratio of Prize Boxes for 5th Cosmo Spins Box) is assigned to her and is 'In Product' awaiting Mike's review. The WC Predictor quiz image assets discussion (group DM) was also in progress with her yesterday. Confirm whether GDCU-8948 review can wait until Monday or if another team member needs to pick up anything urgent. Note: Jessica also asked for feedback on 'the design she did for Colin and Dean' — identify what this refers to and respond early next week.

REVIEW — GDCU-8874 Page Bloat: Reduce HTML payload size (Harden Thomas Wallace, HIGH, In Product). Updated 07/05 14:30. High-priority SEO task — 4 separate fixes identified (CookieYes lazy render, SSR operator list, VWO async, inline scripts). Three mentioned stakeholders: Chloe Christie, Joshua Smith, Gabriel Cornoiu, Dan Smullen. Mike needs to review approach and provide go-ahead on implementation priority. Harden is also carrying GDCU-8955 (Cool Tones Phase 2, In QA) and GDCU-8810 (Blocked). Review today before Harden's queue expands further.

CONFIRM GROUP BETS DESIGN STATUS — Thursday 07/05 was the target for Group Bets design completion (agreed with Chloe 05/05). Check with Miguel: is design done? If yes → schedule KO call with dev team today or Monday. If not → assess impact on 11 June hard deadline and update Chloe. Also confirm Karl check-in happened to verify WC fixture/resulting data is ready.

REVIEW — GDCU-8806 Implement In-App Age Gate 18+ Verification (Carlos Blauth, MEDIUM, In Product). Updated 06/05 15:18. Compliance requirement for both Apple (Guideline 5.3) and Google Play. Carlos has built the gate per spec (yes/no confirm, stores preference, blocks under-18s). Apple reviewers specifically look for this. Review and approve or provide feedback — this is a gate item for app store submission.

GDCU-8619 UNBLOCKED — Operator List Redesign moved from In Product/Blocked to In Progress, priority elevated to HIGH (07/05 14:55). This was Day 22 blocked since 16/04. Confirm with Ciara Carroll: what direction was given at Wednesday's product sync? What is the first milestone/review point now? Ciara has parallel workload (GDCU-8392 WC Cosmo Spins reskin still Backlog). Agree sprint sequencing before end of today.

UNBLOCK GDCU-8810 — Set Up Organisation Level Developer Accounts for Apple/Google (Harden Thomas Wallace, BLOCKED priority, In Progress). Updated 06/05 18:59. This is a prerequisite for app store submission. Identify who owns the org-level Apple Developer/Google Play Console accounts and escalate. Harden cannot progress without them.

BETTING TIPS HUB — GDCU-8907 🔥 Implement the Betting Tips Hub Page (Sanja Budic, BLOCKED priority, In QA). Group DM yesterday: Sanja asked Mike and Gabriel whether to return tips for European leagues — Gabriel confirmed yes. Sanja then said 'This should be fixed now.' Clarify: is the blocker resolved or still open? Ticket still shows Blocked as of 06/05 17:00. If Sanja's fix is in, move to unblocked and confirm QA can proceed.

WC PREDICTOR GAME — GDCU-8935 (Miguel Migneco, Backlog). Design target is Tuesday 12/05. Jessica is off today so the group DM about image assets is paused. Confirm with Miguel: has he started design? Does the Thursday Group Bets work impact his capacity for Predictor this week? If the image asset question from yesterday's DM is still unresolved, provide direction today so Miguel can proceed without waiting for Jessica.

VIC DADSON CHECK-IN — Still no Slack activity from Vic since 21/04. Sick leave should have ended 05/05 but no messages seen this week either. If Vic is back, reach out today — wellbeing first, then data engineering plan (Nemanja requirements + knowledge sharing from Chris in BI team as agreed with Colin 17/04). If Vic is still absent, flag to Colin Brannigan for awareness.

DESIGN SYSTEM GUIDELINES — In yesterday's group DM (Ciara, Miguel, Jessica, Mike), the team flagged a recurring pain point: unclear guidelines on gradient usage, image standards, and Figma assets panel hygiene. Miguel and Jessica both raised this. Asset panel contains conflicting component libraries (Tailwind, GDC DS, Heroicons, Material). Consider scheduling a short team alignment — Mike to provide direction on acceptable design patterns and consolidate the Figma assets panel.

DUPLICATE PAYMENT METHODS — Icelandic EN brand reviews (e.g. gambling.com/is/en/spinlander) showing duplicate payment methods. Chloe raised in #gdc-pod — possibly related to language settings pulling in both variants. Sanja flagged as hotfix candidate or next release. No ticket created yet (Chloe asked Sanja to create one). Confirm ticket is raised and assigned.

INTERVIEW COORDINATION — Liam Caffrey messaged Mike and Gabriel in a group DM looking for a slot Monday or Wednesday next week (12 or 14 May) between 12–3pm. Gabriel said Wednesday works, available 12–3. Liam noted Mike's calendar looks full in that window. Check calendar and either free up a slot or propose an alternative time to Liam.

needs_input.summary: [GDC][Dev][SEO] Page Bloat: Reduce HTML payload size across all markets — CookieYes, SSR operator list, VWO, inline scripts
needs_input.context: Updated 07/05 14:30. High-priority SEO initiative — uk/online-casinos page is 3,789 KB, 29,300 DOM nodes (Google limit: 1,500). Four independent fixes proposed in descending priority: (1) CookieYes lazy render saves 432 KB / 18,900 DOM nodes; (2) SSR operator list saves up to 1,212 KB on listing pages; (3) VWO async load saves 379 KB; (4) inline scripts cleanup. Mike needs to review approach and approve implementation order. Stakeholders tagged: Chloe Christie, Joshua Smith, Gabriel Cornoiu, Dan Smullen.

needs_input.summary: [GDC][UX] Update Ratio of Prize Boxes so a 5th Box Can Appear
needs_input.context: Updated 07/05 09:40. Marketing wants a 5th prize box (£250 Cash Prize) added to the Cosmo Spins landing page. Jessica to share optimal aspect ratio for boxes — boxes are HTML snippets applied to the page. Mike's review needed. NOTE: Jessica is off today (08/05) — review can wait until Monday unless time-sensitive for a campaign deadline.

Updated 06/05 15:18. Compliance requirement for Apple App Store (Guideline 5.3) and Google Play. Implementation: age confirmation on first launch, stores preference locally, blocks under-18s from app content. Works on both Android/iOS. Apple reviewers specifically look for this — approval needed before store submission. Carlos has been in progress since 30/03; this is a significant milestone.
